title: cheilanthes tenuifolia subsp. contigua
intro: Cheilanthes tenuifolia subsp. contigua, also known as the forked rock fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows on rocky outcrops and cliffs. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. Herbal preparations of Cheilanthes tenuifolia subsp. contigua may be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, and it is often consumed as a tea or used in tinctures and infusions.
